response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and web-oriented engineer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and some embedded work, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role on every critical dimension. The most disqualifying factor is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement. Beyond that, he has no demonstrated security engineering background (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, tamper detection), no Linux kernel development or modification experience, and only superficial C usage from a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments. His career trajectory is firmly in web, cloud, and data infrastructure, not embedded networking or security firmware.
strengths: Long career with consistent senior-level responsibility and broad language/platform exposure across embedded, web, and systems contexts
Brief C and C++ experience at National Instruments and in personal projects (Sound Position Server, DSWS) shows at least foundational systems programming exposure
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, clean code) would complement any engineering team's quality standards
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero experience with security technologies required by the role: no MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, MAC authentication, encryption protocols, or attack/tamper detection
No Linux kernel work, no embedded networking stack experience, and no familiarity with switch chips, BIOS/bootloader, NETCONF, SNMP, or chip-vendor SDKs

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking experience
